title: Moira Vs. Town Council

jokes.text: Look at the state of this place, it's like we're in a Mumbai-an slum.
jokes.setup: Wealthy woman forced to live in modest circumstances
jokes.punchline: Comparing their current situation to a Mumbai slum with affected pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira pretentiousness with her affected pronunciation and dramatic comparison. Strong character integration.

jokes.text: Well, the good news is that mattress is covering up the cracked toilet seat.
jokes.setup: Moira complaining about their living conditions
jokes.punchline: Finding the silver lining in one piece of trash hiding another
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of finding positives in increasingly dire circumstances. Great economy.

jokes.text: John, do you remember what Goldie Hawn told us at that Amfar dinner? 'You are the life you accept for yourself.' Those are Goldie's words. Or something someone said to her in India. Or perhaps she read it.
jokes.setup: Moira citing inspirational wisdom from a celebrity
jokes.punchline: The source becomes increasingly vague and uncertain with each revision
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Moira's pretentious name-dropping deflating into uncertainty. Perfect character work.

jokes.text: I mean, would it kill someone to plant a few peonies?!
jokes.setup: Discussion about improving their living situation
jokes.punchline: Moira's entitled expectation that someone else should beautify the town for her
jokes.explanation: Classic entitled Moira expecting others to solve her problems. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: I have a feeling if you want peonies, Moira, you're gonna have to plant them yourself.
jokes.setup: Moira demanding someone plant peonies
jokes.punchline: The obvious solution that horrifies Moira - doing it herself
jokes.explanation: Johnny's practical suggestion sets up Moira's horrified response. Good setup for the payoff.

jokes.text: These are dark times John, but not that dark.
jokes.setup: Johnny suggesting Moira plant flowers herself
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic declaration that manual labor is beyond even their current desperation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira line with excellent economy. The dramatic delivery of refusing manual labor even in poverty is quintessential character comedy.

jokes.text: I love how we can just be silent with each other.
jokes.setup: Two people sitting in comfortable silence
jokes.punchline: Alexis immediately breaks the silence to talk about how nice the silence is
jokes.explanation: Classic irony of talking about not talking. Sets up the relationship dynamic conflict well.

jokes.text: Because now we can just express how we feel through silence.
jokes.setup: Alexis talking about enjoying silence
jokes.punchline: She continues talking extensively about not talking
jokes.explanation: Escalates the irony and shows Alexis's inability to stop talking even when celebrating silence.

jokes.text: It's just, you're talking about how nice it is not to talk, but you're still, talking about it.
jokes.setup: Alexis extensively discussing the beauty of silence
jokes.punchline: Mutt pointing out the obvious contradiction
jokes.explanation: Mutt's deadpan observation of the contradiction. Good straight-man response to Alexis's chatter.

jokes.text: Well, that is an understatement.
jokes.setup: Alexis saying she's not a mute in response to criticism about talking too much
jokes.punchline: Mutt's dry acknowledgment that she's the opposite of mute
jokes.explanation: Good economy and deadpan delivery. Mutt's understated way of saying she talks too much.

jokes.text: I don't need to talk about everything, I'm just saying that, if I stopped talking, then who knows when we're gonna start talking again.
jokes.setup: Alexis defending herself against accusations of talking too much
jokes.punchline: Her fear that if she stops talking, conversation will never resume
jokes.explanation: Shows Alexis's insecurity and need to fill silence. Good character insight delivered through comedy.

jokes.text: Fun? - Porn-y.
jokes.setup: David describing old mannequins as 'fun'
jokes.punchline: Alexis's blunt correction that they look pornographic
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy in the correction. The contrast between David's euphemism and Alexis's directness works well.

jokes.text: Where are the scarves? - Oh, I sold those, too.
jokes.setup: Alexis looking for scarves to pair with knick-knacks
jokes.punchline: David casually revealing he's sold those too
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing David is selling everything. The casual delivery adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Alexander Wang once fired my friend over the smell of his cologne. To Alex's credit, 'Curve Pour Hommes' hasn't been the look since '97.
jokes.setup: Discussion about scent being important for branding
jokes.punchline: David's fashion industry anecdote with specific cologne critique
jokes.explanation: Perfect David character moment with fashion industry name-dropping and specific product knowledge. Shows his background.

jokes.text: No jewelry stands. - No jewelry... - Noooo! No, David! Jewelry stands!
jokes.setup: David listing what he bought, Alexis getting confused about jewelry stands
jokes.punchline: Alexis's increasingly dramatic reaction to the confusion
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment with Alexis's dramatic escalation. Relies on performance more than writing.

jokes.text: You have that striking accent. - Thank you. And I'm sure your family wanted more for you when they immigrated from... Winnipeg. - Winnipeg.
jokes.setup: Moira complimenting Ray's 'striking accent'
jokes.punchline: Her assumption he's an exotic immigrant, revealed to be from Winnipeg
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of expectations. Moira's pretentious assumptions deflated by the mundane reality.

jokes.text: I'm hoping to take my complaint to council, and I've been told you'd be the easiest one to crack.
jokes.setup: Moira asking Ray to help with her complaint
jokes.punchline: Her blunt admission that she was told he'd be the pushover
jokes.explanation: Moira's tactless honesty is perfectly in character. The directness of the insult works well.

jokes.text: Too kind.
jokes.setup: Moira saying he'd be the easiest to crack
jokes.punchline: Ray's dry, polite response to being called a pushover
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and deadpan delivery. Ray's polite Canadian response to an insult.

jokes.text: Which won't really help, since I resigned. Um, and council wasn't too happy about it. I'm surprised you didn't hear, I talked about it extensively on my podcast.
jokes.setup: Ray offering to help Moira with council
jokes.punchline: Multiple reveals: he's not on council, they're mad at him, and he has a podcast no one listens to
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with multiple reveals that undermine his helpfulness. The podcast detail is perfect Ray.

jokes.text: Look at us, son, a couple of working stiffs back from the salt mines. - What?! - Two men, back from the mines.
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to bond with David using folksy expression
jokes.punchline: David's complete confusion at the outdated phrase
jokes.explanation: Good generational comedy with Johnny's outdated expressions confusing David. Sets up the extended bit.

jokes.text: The salt mines! Back from when people mined. When miners worked in the, salt industry, they'd come home, after digging... salt. I don't know what it means!
jokes.setup: David not understanding 'salt mines' expression
jokes.punchline: Johnny's increasingly desperate and circular explanation ending in admission of ignorance
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Johnny's attempt to explain something he doesn't understand himself. Great character moment.

jokes.text: Uh-huh, you know I just got back from a four-hour shift that mostly consisted of me sampling luxury candles so...
jokes.setup: Johnny's working man bonding attempt
jokes.punchline: David's dry description of his extremely unmanual 'labor'
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast to Johnny's 'salt mines' with David's bougie version of work. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: There's nothing in here. - Really? - Yeah. - Oh, I must've taken the last one.
jokes.setup: Johnny offering David a beer for his hard work
jokes.punchline: Empty fridge reveals Johnny drank the last beer
jokes.explanation: Simple setup-punchline showing Johnny's selfishness despite his generous gesture. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I mean, you got this job all on your own, nobody had to make a call, nobody got involved, nobody had to... pay somebody off. - What?
jokes.setup: Johnny praising David for getting a job on his own merit
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific details about how Johnny usually had to intervene, ending with bribery
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing Johnny's past interventions. David's shocked 'What?' sells the punchline.

jokes.text: Well, word to the wise, son, don't... spend all your money at once. - I thought you weren't getting involved. - No, I'm not. I'm not, I'm just saying, word to the wise, is all.
jokes.setup: Johnny promising not to interfere with David's life
jokes.punchline: Immediately giving unsolicited financial advice then denying he's interfering
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency showing Johnny can't help but give advice. The immediate contradiction works well.

jokes.text: Okay, what does 'word to the wise' even mean? Word to the wise. When you're... speaking to somebody wise, you lis... you're cognizant of what they're saying. A wise person telling you... Anyway, just-just uh, keep that in mind
jokes.setup: David asking for explanation of another expression
jokes.punchline: Johnny's second failed attempt to explain a phrase he uses but doesn't understand
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the salt mines bit. Shows Johnny consistently uses expressions he can't explain.

jokes.text: Honestly, honestly, I have no money, and an empty purse from two seasons ago!
jokes.setup: Alexis thinking someone is robbing her
jokes.punchline: Her privileged concerns about being poor
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Alexis's version of being broke involves designer accessories

jokes.text: Oh my God! Your face! Yes, you can see it now! You shaved!
jokes.setup: Mutt appearing without his beard
jokes.punchline: Alexis's dramatic reaction to seeing his actual face for the first time
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment with Alexis's typical dramatic reaction. Sets up the relationship conflict.

jokes.text: You'd have said, uh, 'wow Alexis, I'm thinking about shaving my beard.' And then I would've said, 'hm, no mutt, I don't think that that's the right journey for you at this point in time.'
jokes.setup: Discussion about communication in relationships
jokes.punchline: Alexis's valley girl speak about a 'journey' for basic grooming decisions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis character moment with her therapy speak about a 'journey' for shaving. Very quotable.

jokes.text: I was a beard for a very well known actor, and I get it, it's uncomfortable.
jokes.setup: Discussion about the difficulty of having a beard
jokes.punchline: Alexis's casual mention of being a beard (fake girlfriend) for a closeted actor
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment revealing Alexis's past through casual reference. Great double meaning with beard/facial hair.

jokes.text: My beard is your favorite thing about me! - I said one of my favorite things. - No, no, no, no. You said, 'favorite thing!'
jokes.setup: Argument about what Alexis said about his beard
jokes.punchline: Mutt's devastation that facial hair was her favorite thing, and her backtracking
jokes.explanation: Good relationship cringe moment. Mutt's hurt feelings over the beard being her favorite thing is relatable.

jokes.text: Look at your little doll lips.
jokes.setup: Seeing Mutt's face without beard for first time
jokes.punchline: Alexis's condescending but affectionate description of his newly visible lips
jokes.explanation: Good economy with Alexis's typical condescending endearment. The 'doll lips' description is perfectly her.

jokes.text: I've tried it twice, and both times, I've been sort of shot back into traffic.
jokes.setup: Discussing the new ramp at the post office being too steep
jokes.punchline: The ramp launches wheelchair users backward into traffic
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy with absurd consequence of poorly designed accessibility. Good dark humor.

jokes.text: I was just hoping to be interrupted by a concerned citizen.
jokes.setup: Moira bursting into council meeting
jokes.punchline: Roland's sarcastic response to the unwelcome interruption
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic delivery from Roland. Shows his attitude toward Moira's dramatics.

jokes.text: It's like a birthday streamer. You-you-it stretches, and then, and then you pull it, and it just snaps, okay?
jokes.setup: Johnny complaining about cheap toilet paper
jokes.punchline: Detailed physical description comparing toilet paper to party streamers
jokes.explanation: Functional observational humor about cheap toilet paper. The comparison is apt but not exceptional.

jokes.text: That's why I came in with something funny.
jokes.setup: Johnny realizing Stevie heard his toilet paper rant
jokes.punchline: His awkward admission that he was trying to be funny
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Johnny's awkwardness. Meta-commentary on his failed humor attempt.

jokes.text: I was gonna leave, but now I don't want to.
jokes.setup: Johnny and David arguing about write-offs and purchases
jokes.punchline: Stevie's dry admission she's staying to watch the drama
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie line with excellent economy. Her deadpan enjoyment of family drama is spot-on.

jokes.text: Uh yeah, it's when you buy something for your business and the government pays you back for it. - Oh, and who pays for it? - Nobody, you write it off.
jokes.setup: Discussion about what a write-off means
jokes.punchline: David's complete misunderstanding that write-offs mean free money from government
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup-punchline showing David's entitled misunderstanding of basic business concepts. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Who writes it off?! - I don't know, the govern... The 'write off' people!
jokes.setup: Johnny pressing David on who actually pays for write-offs
jokes.punchline: David's increasingly vague and nonsensical explanation ending with 'write off people'
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of David's ignorance. 'The write off people' is perfectly absurd and quotable.

jokes.text: If I have acne, what does that say about the legitimacy of the store?
jokes.setup: David justifying skincare purchases as business expense
jokes.punchline: His dramatic logic that his complexion reflects on store credibility
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing David's vanity and self-importance. The logic is absurd but fits him.

jokes.text: There's not enough space in here for the massage chair, anyway.
jokes.setup: David saying he'll return some things
jokes.punchline: Casual mention he bought a massage chair, escalating the extravagance
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation revealing David's most extravagant purchase. Good economy in the reveal.

jokes.text: A baby born in a prison cell does not know that a toilet should not be in the same room as its bed, but we do.
jokes.setup: Moira making a point about standards
jokes.punchline: Her bizarre analogy about prison babies and toilet placement
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira absurdist metaphor. The prison baby analogy is perfectly bizarre and quotable.

jokes.text: I didn't know you'd been in prison.
jokes.setup: Moira's prison baby analogy
jokes.punchline: Council member's literal interpretation of her metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response to Moira's absurd analogy. Great economy and timing.

jokes.text: That we should strive for something that sets us apart from the prison baby.
jokes.setup: Moira trying to clarify her confusing metaphor
jokes.punchline: Her continued commitment to the prison baby comparison
jokes.explanation: Good callback escalating the absurd metaphor. 'Sets us apart from the prison baby' is very quotable.

jokes.text: So there was just one baby in prison, or there were more?
jokes.setup: Continued confusion about Moira's prison baby metaphor
jokes.punchline: Council member asking for clarification on the number of prison babies
jokes.explanation: Good callback continuing the literal interpretation. The specificity of asking about numbers is funny.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, did you just ask for cake? I'm not used to you ordering solid food.
jokes.setup: Mutt ordering cake
jokes.punchline: Twyla's observation that he never eats solid food
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Mutt's eating habits. Twyla's directness works well.

jokes.text: Like maybe I need to talk less and listen more, and you need to talk more, and listen less.
jokes.setup: Alexis and Mutt discussing relationship adjustments
jokes.punchline: Her practical but telling assessment of their communication issues
jokes.explanation: Good relationship insight delivered through comedy. Shows Alexis's self-awareness and Mutt's reticence.

jokes.text: I once got Winnie Mandela to RSVP to an 'Artists Against Eczema' benefit within the hour.
jokes.setup: Moira boasting about getting things done quickly
jokes.punchline: Her bizarre name-drop about Winnie Mandela and a ridiculous cause
jokes.explanation: Excellent Moira moment combining pretentious name-dropping with an absurd cause. Very quotable and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: You're a pain in the ass, but you get stuff done.
jokes.setup: Roland observing Moira's effectiveness despite her dramatics
jokes.punchline: His blunt but accurate assessment of her personality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roland line with excellent economy. His crude but honest assessment of Moira works well.

jokes.text: Yes, you try enjoying a puff pastry with everyone around you scratching.
jokes.setup: Reference to the eczema benefit
jokes.punchline: Moira's vivid and disgusting description of the event's atmosphere
jokes.explanation: Great callback with vivid imagery. The contrast between elegant puff pastries and scratching is perfect.

jokes.text: Like, amazing! There was this time in the barn where I climbed up onto this rafter...
jokes.setup: Alexis and David discussing her breakup with Mutt
jokes.punchline: Alexis starting to share explicit sexual details before catching herself
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alexis's tendency to overshare. The abrupt stop works well.

jokes.text: Like, I've been to doctors about it.
jokes.setup: Alexis saying she's never felt anything after breakups
jokes.punchline: Her casual mention of seeking medical help for lack of emotions
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Alexis's emotional detachment. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I think I need a hug, or something. - W-you what? - I need a hug.
jokes.setup: Alexis feeling emotional after the breakup
jokes.punchline: David's shock that his sister is asking for physical affection
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing the family's emotional distance. David's shock at a hug request is perfect.

jokes.text: Did you get new bedding? - Um, yeah, I did. But it's a write off, so I don't...
jokes.setup: Alexis noticing David's new bedding during their hug
jokes.punchline: David still not understanding write-offs after the whole argument
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing David still doesn't understand write-offs. The timing during the hug is nice.

jokes.text: Oh, did you pick up that random condom that was on the grass there? - Ew yeah, whose was that?
jokes.setup: Family gathering to see Moira's improvements
jokes.punchline: Casual discussion of random condom on their property
jokes.explanation: Visual gag about their seedy living situation. The casual discussion of it works as gross-out humor.

jokes.text: Weren't those here before? - There was literally nothing here. It was a wasteland.
jokes.setup: David questioning if the plants were always there
jokes.punchline: Johnny's dramatic description of the previous barren state
jokes.explanation: Shows David's obliviousness and Johnny's dramatic tendencies. Functional family dynamic humor.

jokes.text: Oh, there's the condom right there, David. - Ew! Eww!
jokes.setup: Previous discussion of random condom
jokes.punchline: Johnny pointing it out again, David's disgusted reaction
jokes.explanation: Callback to the condom gag. Simple gross-out humor with David's reaction. Functional ending beat.

tmdb_overview: Moira tries to beautify the town. David misuses the company credit card. A grooming misstep forces Alexis and Mutt to question their relationship.
insight: Moira's town council showdown generates 153 jokes across 57 minutes—deadpan delivery carries a middling score.
